content.text: Employment history received. References checked. Most of them answered.
A résumé is who you were before the contract. It grants exactly three things: two skills at Tier 1, a standing line, and your starting credits.
Two skill Tier 1s. Each is the full tier: the trained dice (The Two Rolls) plus a pick of one of the tier's two features (The Twelve Skills).
A standing line. Position in the world — established fiction the table honors. A standing line is never a roll bonus: résumés grant standing and leverage, never numbers.
Starting credits. Printed in the table below and nowhere else — the Finishing checklist points back here.

rows: Every fence knows your face, kindly
One gang owes you; one remembers you
Your old badge still opens lobby doors
One Leverage tag on a named corp officer — and theirs on you
You can always find water, shelter, and a buyer

content.label: DESIGN GAP
content.text: The Disgraced Executive starts holding a Leverage tag — and being held by one. Leverage tags are created and spent by Negotiate features (The Twelve Skills), but the tag's own rules — what holding one does, and what it costs to be on the wrong end of one — are not printed in the rebuild docs.
Gaps in employment history are normal and nothing to be ashamed of. They have been independently filled in by our records department.
